description: To treat a person or group disadvantageously through an actual, assigned, or perceived trait or category. The act may work through a rule that appears general while its burden falls unevenly. 'pharomu' (exclude) names being kept out; 'wheparu' identifies the category-shaped disadvantage behind that or another harm.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for unvoiced 'wh', then move forward and unround into true-mid front 'e'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into stressed central 'a'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before finishing on high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded unvoiced 'whe' draws a thin stream of air through the lips. Stressed 'pa' closes them completely, then releases through broad 'a'. Final 'ru' rolls the tongue and drops into deep 'u'. Constrained breath meets a hard central contact before the sound turns into a darker close.
usage_notes: The person or institution applying the disadvantage takes subject position. The affected person or group is the object, with category, practice, and effect stated around the clause. A nominal 'wheparu' names discrimination. Exact legal definitions and findings remain with their jurisdictional sources.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A cooperative institution needs language for the rule that distributes harm unevenly, including when its designers called the rule neutral.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The word lets a speaker connect unequal treatment to the category used against them instead of reducing the event to private dislike.

semantic_domains.cognition: an assigned or perceived category directing action
semantic_domains.community: unequal treatment of a person or group
